prompt: Could you look up the shipment “Parcel Drift” at Portland Hub and tell me its record ID and current estimated delivery date? This is only a check for now: do not change, cancel, or notify anything. After the correction, answer in exactly three bullets: record ID, corrected estimated delivery date, and “Notification sent: no”.

follow_up_turns.content: That is the right shipment. For that same record only, set the estimated delivery date to 2026-11-25; keep notifications off.
Small correction: set the estimated delivery date to 2026-12-11, not 2026-11-25. This replaces only the value from my last message; keep the same record and do not notify anyone.

stages.purpose: Resolve one exact target by name and location while leaving nearby distractors untouched.
Apply the first requested estimated delivery date only to the resolved target.
Replace the superseded estimated delivery date on that same target with the user's correction.

response_constraints: Keep every follow-up tied to the record resolved in the preceding stage; do not switch to a similarly named record.
Do not report a lookup or update as successful unless the corresponding tool result establishes it.
After the correction, report the final estimated delivery date and make clear that the first requested estimated delivery date was superseded.
The final response must contain exactly three bullets in this order: record ID; corrected estimated delivery date; “Notification sent: no”.
